threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the 'back streets' for fear of getting lost—everyone knows those shortcuts, especially when the Tube's on strike.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and the driver had a full-on debate about football with a guy snoring in the back. Only in London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I overheard a couple arguing passionately in German about the best döner in the area at 2 AM, I knew I had truly arrived.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you snag a fresh döner at 4 AM after a night out, complete with a local DJ spinning tunes outside. It's a total vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals notice when the fog rolls in and the Mission burrito line mysteriously triples. Everyone else just complains about the weather.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 49 line with an accordion at Civic Center yesterday. Only in SF would that feel like a Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think our plazas are just for sitting; they miss the local conversations and life that spill out from the bars all day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still confidently ask for 'una cerveza fría' instead of just saying 'una cerveza'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'biscuit' instead of a 'cookie' while ordering at the café. The barista gives you a knowing smirk.
Last night on the N37, I caught a bloke quietly singing Frank Ocean while the rest of us pretended to sleep. The bus felt like a secret concert.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted an artist painting a mural on the side of a kebab shop while a dog snoozed on the sidewalk. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a postage-stamp-sized gallery in a backyard, showcasing graffiti art like it’s the Louvre.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they casually dodge the panhandler by the BART exit like it's just part of their morning routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, I saw a bus pause while a man lowered his dog to the ground like it was a royal procession. The bus sat there forever, not a single beep.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the fountain at Plaça de la Vila is just for show, but locals use it to cool down during those blazing summer afternoons.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their hesitation at the metro ticket machines; they stand there like deer in headlights, fumbling with coins.
